title: Capturing the resilience dividend in the Pacific: dissemination webinar on global infrastructure resilience 2025 (GIR 2025) report
content: As part of its ongoing efforts to disseminate the key messages, CDRI is organizing a dedicated webinar with the Pacific Region Infrastructure Facility (PRIF) to present the key findings, insights, and frameworks from GIR 2025.

title: Using data in disaster and humanitarian studies
content: Join the Humanitarian Studies Centre team and renowned invited speakers for a webinar on the use of data in its many forms in humanitarian and disaster studies
snippet: Join the Humanitarian Studies Centre team and renowned invited speakers for a webinar on the use of data in its many forms in humanitarian and disaster studies

title: Redefining climate resilience: How RSS-Hydro is predicting weather impact with AI
content: As climate resilience becomes a critical pillar of European policies, RSS-Hydro’s tools utilise cutting edge geospatial technology to forecast weather and impact in immersive detail.
snippet: As climate resilience becomes a critical pillar of European policies, RSS-Hydro’s tools utilise cutting edge geospatial technology to forecast weather and impact in immersive detail.

title: Summer course on wildfire research
content: Discover how wildfire behavior modelling, fire safety strategies, and advanced technological tools can support risk assessment and management.
snippet: Discover how wildfire behavior modelling, fire safety strategies, and advanced technological tools can support risk assessment and management.

title: Identifying severe weather hazards further in the future with AI
content: Experimental forecasts leverage strengths of AI weather models to extend the forecast

title: Australian Journal of Emergency Management (Vol. 41, No. 1, January 2026)
content: This issue celebrates the 40 years of publication of the Australian Journal of Emergency Management (AJEM).
snippet: This issue celebrates the 40 years of publication of the Australian Journal of Emergency Management (AJEM).

title: Australian Journal of Emergency Management (Vol. 41, No. 2, April 2026)
content: This issue provides a historical and critical overview of the development of emergency management in Australia from the 1970s to the present.
snippet: This issue provides a historical and critical overview of the development of emergency management in Australia from the 1970s to the present.

title: Cities are rehearsing for deadly heat. Will it help when disaster comes?
content: As heat waves grow longer and deadlier, cities around the world are using drills and tabletop exercises to expose weaknesses before a real emergency strikes.
snippet: As heat waves grow longer and deadlier, cities around the world are using drills and tabletop exercises to expose weaknesses before a real emergency strikes.

title: Scientists map the hidden electrical structure of the U.S., revealing new clues about Earth's interior and potential risks to the power grid
content: The work represents the first United States Magnetotelluric Array (USMTArray) comprehensive survey of the electrical properties beneath the continent.
snippet: The work represents the first United States Magnetotelluric Array (USMTArray) comprehensive survey of the electrical properties beneath the continent.

title: Louisiana’s shrinking coast offers a narrowing window for managed retreat
content: Louisiana is losing its coast faster than anywhere else in the U.S. What happens next could become a blueprint — or a warning — for vulnerable communities around the globe.
snippet: Louisiana is losing its coast faster than anywhere else in the U.S. What happens next could become a blueprint — or a warning — for vulnerable communities around the globe.

title: Want to predict wildfire severity? Look to the state of vegetation
content: A study connects satellite data on vegetation condition, topography, and weather conditions to examine the predicted versus actual burn severity of the 2025 Los Angeles wildfires.
snippet: A study connects satellite data on vegetation condition, topography, and weather conditions to examine the predicted versus actual burn severity of the 2025 Los Angeles wildfires.

title: Climate disasters don’t just destroy homes, they change lives forever. We spoke with cyclone survivors in Zimbabwe
content: Some of the most profound impacts of climate-induced disasters are emotional, cultural and social, affecting how people feel, relate to each other and think about their world.
snippet: Some of the most profound impacts of climate-induced disasters are emotional, cultural and social, affecting how people feel, relate to each other and think about their world.

title: Tulane researchers say Louisiana could lead global climate adaptation efforts
content: Louisiana is in a unique position to build expertise and infrastructure that will be the foundation for coming generations. Transition planning offers economic opportunities, from land-building strategies to renewable energy and new housing development

title: Research reveals repeated flooding is altering a key Florida natural resource
content: The study shows that the impacts of flooding extend beyond individual events sustaining darker water, lower oxygen levels, and accelerating the loss of underwater plants, the perfect recipe for harmful algal growth.

title: Bahrain National Platform
content: Bahrain launched a National Civil Protection Platform led by the Ministry of Interior. It is described as an integrated national initiative to enhance public safety, raise risk awareness, and deliver timely information to citizens and residents. The platform's vision is to cultivate a culture of safety across the Kingdom and strengthen its emergency strategy by sharing knowledge, alerts, guidelines, and risk information. It is anchored in Bahrain’s National Emergency Strategy and aligned with crisis management and civil defence mechanisms.

title: Towards science-based climate policy in Kerala: Integrating adaptation and mitigation for a climate-resilient future
content: This article examines the urgent need for science-based climate policy in Kerala, India, where increasing heat stress, extreme rainfall, floods, landslides and droughts are intensifying climate risks.
snippet: This article examines the urgent need for science-based climate policy in Kerala, India, where increasing heat stress, extreme rainfall, floods, landslides and droughts are intensifying climate risks.

title: Call for bids: Host EURESFO27
content: Cities and regional governments are invited to express their interest in hosting the 14th European Urban Resilience Forum (EURESFO) in 2027 - a unique opportunity to place your city at the heart of Europe’s urban resilience conversation.

title: Engaging communities in climate vulnerability assessment: lessons from Southern Africa
content: This study shares lessons from implementing community-level participatory climate vulnerability assessments processes in nearly 100 communities in Mozambique, Zambia, and Zimbabwe from 2024 to 2025.
snippet: This study shares lessons from implementing community-level participatory climate vulnerability assessments processes in nearly 100 communities in Mozambique, Zambia, and Zimbabwe from 2024 to 2025.

title: Sudan National Platform
content: The National Council for Civil Defense has been the focal point for disaster risk management in Sudan since its establishment in 1994. Its membership includes all sovereign ministries, states, and other relevant entities. The Council is chaired by the Minister of the Interior and has a General Secretariat headed by the Director of the Civil Defense Forces.

title: Somalia National Platform
content: On 13 December 2021, the Government of Somalia launched the National Platform for Disaster Risk Reduction (NPDRR).The NPDRR in Somalia brings together various stakeholders that are responsible for facilitating disaster risk reduction efforts in the country in alignment with the Sendai Framework for Disaster Risk Reduction and in coherence with other global frameworks.

title: Jordan National Platform
content: In 2015, a Royal Decree was issued with the approval of the National Center for Security and Crises Management bylaw, No. (20), and became effective as of April 1, 2015. It includes the responsibilities and duties expected from the establishment of the national platform for DRR.

title: Preparatory phase of large earthquakes illuminated by unsupervised categorization of earthquake catalog features
content: This study introduces an unsupervised machine learning framework to categorize seismicity patterns and identify, when present, seismicity transients preceding large earthquakes.
snippet: This study introduces an unsupervised machine learning framework to categorize seismicity patterns and identify, when present, seismicity transients preceding large earthquakes.

title: The Gambia country climate and development report
content: This Country Climate and Development Report (CCDR) demonstrates that climate-informed development is essential to sustaining growth and protecting national assets.
snippet: This Country Climate and Development Report (CCDR) demonstrates that climate-informed development is essential to sustaining growth and protecting national assets.

title: Wildfires leave lasting risks: improving predictions of hazardous debris flows in burned areas
content: Destructive landslides are more likely to occur after wildfires, but how does the hazard change as vegetation regrows? Scientists are improving prediction methods to reduce costly unnecessary warnings
snippet: Destructive landslides are more likely to occur after wildfires, but how does the hazard change as vegetation regrows? Scientists are improving prediction methods to reduce costly unnecessary warnings

title: When the Schuylkill swallowed the city: Lessons from Hurricane Ida’s historic flood
content: Penn research shows that Hurricane Ida wasn’t a once-in-a-century anomaly but a preview of how climate change, urbanization, and aging infrastructure are rewriting flood risk.
snippet: Penn research shows that Hurricane Ida wasn’t a once-in-a-century anomaly but a preview of how climate change, urbanization, and aging infrastructure are rewriting flood risk.

title: Financial strain, lockdowns and fear of infection during disease outbreaks magnify violence against women and girls − research
content: When the world shut down due to the COVID-19 pandemic in early 2020, another crisis quietly grew behind closed doors.
snippet: When the world shut down due to the COVID-19 pandemic in early 2020, another crisis quietly grew behind closed doors.

title: ‘I’m mad at the people who could have solved the problem’: what kids told us about eco‑anxiety
content: Researchers interviewed 15 Australian primary school children aged between nine and 12-years-old about environmental change, which includes things such as pollution, climate change and deforestation.
snippet: Researchers interviewed 15 Australian primary school children aged between nine and 12-years-old about environmental change, which includes things such as pollution, climate change and deforestation.

title: Targeting green space accessibility gaps to mitigate urban heat island risk in rapidly urbanizing megacities
content: This study integrates urban heat island (UHI) risk assessment with green space accessibility analysis to locate mismatches between heat risk mitigation need and green provision, and identifies the most vulnerable populations.

title: Seasonal predictions and their applications in the Mediterranean Region: Part I—sources of predictability and prediction skill
content: The main objective of this paper is to review and discuss the current understanding of climate variability and sources of predictability in the Mediterranean basin and surrounding areas.
snippet: The main objective of this paper is to review and discuss the current understanding of climate variability and sources of predictability in the Mediterranean basin and surrounding areas.

title: Seasonal predictions and their applications in the Mediterranean Region: Part II—prediction-based services
content: This review paper examines the development and application of climate predictions, primarily at seasonal timescales, as actionable information for decision-making, with a specific focus on the Mediterranean region.

title: Taiwan disaster management reference handbook
content: This handbook provides an overview of the hazards, disaster management structure and key actors, and other considerations to support planners, practitioners, and responders in understanding Taiwan's disaster management environment.

title: Climate change is supercharging hurricane rainfall, contributing to deadly floods
content: Freshwater flooding has caused over half of all direct hurricane deaths since 2013. The threat will increase as climate change makes hurricanes wetter and potentially slower-moving.
snippet: Freshwater flooding has caused over half of all direct hurricane deaths since 2013. The threat will increase as climate change makes hurricanes wetter and potentially slower-moving.

title: Alaska’s near‑record landslide tsunami sent a wave 1,580 feet up the fjord walls – and left clues for building a warning system
content: On the evening of Aug. 9, 2025, passengers on the Hanse Explorer were taking photos and videos of the South Sawyer Glacier. Twelve hours later, a landslide collapsed into the fjord, initiating the second-highest tsunami in recorded history.

title: Liminal diplomacy at the poles: Japan's disaster risk reduction and the making of Arctic order
content: The Arctic has reemerged as a dynamic yet unsettled governance arena.
snippet: The Arctic has reemerged as a dynamic yet unsettled governance arena.

title: Early climate health investments generate 68-fold gains in low- and middle-income countries
content: World Resources Institute and The Rockefeller Foundation today announced new research, which finds that early investment in climate health solutions in low- and middle-income countries can yield between US$4 and US$68 in benefits for every US$1 invested.

title: 'Indian Niño' drove record heat in 2023, 2024, new study finds
content: In 2023 and 2024, Earth’s average surface temperature spiked nearly 0.3 degrees Celsius above what was already expected from climate change. Each year was declared the hottest and coincided with deadly wildfires, heat waves and climate-related disasters.

title: Alaska’s 2025 mega tsunami highlights risk to cruise lines as glaciers retreat
content: Researchers say 481-metre wave in fjord was triggered by rockslide linked to climate crisis.
snippet: Researchers say 481-metre wave in fjord was triggered by rockslide linked to climate crisis.

title: The 6th episode in the series ADPC’s Young Professional Platform – “Interns Talk”
content: This webinar aims to explore how risk assessment can be used to effectively identify, assess, and implement Nature-based Solutions for urban flood risk reduction.
snippet: This webinar aims to explore how risk assessment can be used to effectively identify, assess, and implement Nature-based Solutions for urban flood risk reduction.

title: Fire is transforming the US West’s public lands – research shows overlooked cost to recreation
content: The scars left by the 2020 Cameron Peak and East Troublesome fires remain stark: blackened hillsides, closed trails and bare slopes where forests once stood. Tthe fires caused significant and lasting declines in visitation at the burned sites.

title: Sharks may help improve climate forecasts, study finds
content: Research demonstrates how animal-borne sensors can work in tandem with traditional tools to improve ocean predictions.
snippet: Research demonstrates how animal-borne sensors can work in tandem with traditional tools to improve ocean predictions.

title: Dangerous heavy rains are getting more likely and widespread
content: Seven of the top 11 highest-volume precipitation events over the past 77 years have occurred just in the past 10 years.
snippet: Seven of the top 11 highest-volume precipitation events over the past 77 years have occurred just in the past 10 years.
